cover.title: where AI helps depends on your code
cover.sub: Stanford studied 100,000+ developers across 600+ companies. The gains split hard by where the work happens.

slides.title: 35-40% on greenfield
slides.body: Clean, low-complexity, new code. This is the number the demos show you.

slides.title: single digits on brownfield
slides.body: 0-10% on the complex, legacy code where most teams actually live. Some teams went net-negative as rework ate the gain.

slides.title: one case: 2.5x more rework
slides.body: The speedup got eaten and then some. Adoption is not improvement when it's pointed at the wrong code.

slides.title: the lever is your codebase
slides.body: Net AI productivity rose with test coverage, types, docs, and modularity. The state of your code, which you can change.

cta.title: find where AI is costing you
cta.sub: A straight read of your agent setup in your real repo, sources and method included. The prioritized plan is yours to keep.
